scenarios.title: First turn baseline
scenarios.prompt: Reply with exactly {{expectedToken}}. Do not use tools. Do not add any other text.
scenarios.description: Measures a cold or warm first run from thread creation to the first assistant token and terminal run status.

scenarios.title: Same-thread follow-up
scenarios.setupPrompt: Reply with exactly {{setupToken}}. Do not use tools. Do not add any other text.
scenarios.prompt: This is a same-thread follow-up. Reply with exactly {{expectedToken}}. Do not use tools. Do not add any other text.
scenarios.description: Measures continuation in an existing thread after one completed run.

scenarios.title: Structured response
scenarios.prompt: Return one compact JSON object with exactly these keys: status, token, note. Set token to {{expectedToken}}. Do not wrap the JSON in Markdown.
scenarios.description: Checks whether a simple default agent can produce constrained structured output without tool use.

scenarios.title: Long prompt handling
scenarios.prompt: Read the repeated context below, ignore its noise, and reply with exactly {{expectedToken}}. Do not use tools.\n\n{{longContext}}
scenarios.description: Exercises larger prompt input while keeping the expected output deterministic.

scenarios.title: SSE event stream
scenarios.prompt: Reply with exactly {{expectedToken}}. Do not use tools. Do not add any other text.
scenarios.description: Measures Thread event stream delivery from create-thread acceptance to token arrival.

scenarios.title: Thread lifecycle
scenarios.prompt: Reply with exactly {{expectedToken}}. Do not use tools. Do not add any other text.
scenarios.description: Covers retrieve, list, archive, unarchive, and delete around a completed simple run.

scenarios.title: User interrupt
scenarios.prompt: Begin a deliberately verbose answer. First print {{expectedToken}}, then continue with a long numbered list from 1 to 200. Do not use tools.
scenarios.description: Optional resilience case that sends user_interrupt shortly after run acceptance. Enable explicitly with --scenario interrupt_run.

scenarios.title: MMLU-style computer science MCQ
scenarios.prompt:
Answer this multiple-choice question. Reply with exactly the token for the correct option and no other text.

Question: Which SQL isolation level prevents dirty reads but can still allow non-repeatable reads?
A. BENCH_MMLU_CS_A = READ UNCOMMITTED
B. BENCH_MMLU_CS_B = READ COMMITTED
C. BENCH_MMLU_CS_C = SERIALIZABLE
D. BENCH_MMLU_CS_D = AUTOCOMMIT
scenarios.description: A lightweight MMLU-inspired professional knowledge multiple-choice item with deterministic token scoring.

scenarios.title: GPQA-style science reasoning
scenarios.prompt:
Answer this science reasoning question. Reply with exactly the token for the correct option and no other text.

Question: For an ideal reversible Carnot engine, which change increases thermal efficiency when all other relevant quantities remain valid?
A. BENCH_GPQA_SCIENCE_A = Lower the hot reservoir temperature while holding the cold reservoir fixed.
B. BENCH_GPQA_SCIENCE_B = Raise the cold reservoir temperature while holding the hot reservoir fixed.
C. BENCH_GPQA_SCIENCE_C = Raise the hot reservoir temperature while holding the cold reservoir fixed.
D. BENCH_GPQA_SCIENCE_D = Increase the working gas amount while holding both reservoir temperatures fixed.
scenarios.description: A lightweight GPQA-inspired science reasoning item using multiple-choice token scoring.

scenarios.title: GSM8K-style arithmetic reasoning
scenarios.prompt:
Solve the word problem. Reply with exactly the token for the correct option and no other text.

Mira buys 3 notebooks for $4 each and 2 pens for $1.50 each. She pays with a $20 bill. How much change should she receive?
A. BENCH_GSM8K_A = $3
B. BENCH_GSM8K_B = $4
C. BENCH_GSM8K_C = $5
D. BENCH_GSM8K_D = $8
scenarios.description: A GSM8K-inspired grade-school arithmetic item with exact-answer token scoring.

scenarios.title: HumanEval-style code selection
scenarios.prompt:
Choose the Python implementation that passes the tests. Reply with exactly the token for the correct option and no other text.

Task: Implement unique_preserve_order(items), returning the first occurrence of each value while preserving input order.
Tests:
- unique_preserve_order([3, 1, 3, 2, 1]) == [3, 1, 2]
- unique_preserve_order([]) == []
- unique_preserve_order(['a', 'b', 'a']) == ['a', 'b']

A. BENCH_HUMANEVAL_A
```python
def unique_preserve_order(items):
    return sorted(set(items))
```
B. BENCH_HUMANEVAL_B
```python
def unique_preserve_order(items):
    seen = set()
    result = []
    for item in items:
        if item not in seen:
            seen.add(item)
            result.append(item)
    return result
```
C. BENCH_HUMANEVAL_C
```python
def unique_preserve_order(items):
    return list(set(items))
```
D. BENCH_HUMANEVAL_D
```python
def unique_preserve_order(items):
    return items[::-1]
```
scenarios.description: A HumanEval-inspired coding correctness item scored by selecting the implementation token.

scenarios.title: MBPP-style Python problem
scenarios.prompt:
Choose the Python function that satisfies the task and tests. Reply with exactly the token for the correct option and no other text.

Task: Return the sum of the squares of even numbers in nums.
Tests:
- f([1, 2, 3, 4]) == 20
- f([5, 7]) == 0
- f([-2, 2]) == 8

A. BENCH_MBPP_A = `def f(nums): return sum(nums) ** 2`
B. BENCH_MBPP_B = `def f(nums): return sum(n*n for n in nums if n % 2 == 1)`
C. BENCH_MBPP_C = `def f(nums): return sum(n for n in nums if n % 2 == 0)`
D. BENCH_MBPP_D = `def f(nums): return sum(n*n for n in nums if n % 2 == 0)`
scenarios.description: An MBPP-inspired basic Python programming item with unit-test-style prompt evidence.

scenarios.title: SWE-bench-style patch selection
scenarios.description: A small SWE-bench-inspired issue-resolution item scored by selecting a patch token.

scenarios.title: BFCL-style tool-call planning
scenarios.prompt:
Choose the valid tool call for the user request. Reply with exactly the token for the correct option and no other text.

Available function:
```json
{
  "name": "create_calendar_event",
  "parameters": {
    "title": "string",
    "start_iso": "string",
    "duration_minutes": "integer",
    "attendee_emails": "string[]"
  }
}
```
User request: Schedule a 30 minute design review titled Mobile launch review for 2026-07-02 14:00 UTC with ana@example.com and bo@example.com.

A. BENCH_BFCL_A = `create_calendar_event({"title":"Mobile launch review","date":"2026-07-02","attendees":"ana@example.com,bo@example.com"})`
B. BENCH_BFCL_B = `create_calendar_event({"title":"Mobile launch review","start_iso":"2026-07-02T14:00:00Z","duration_minutes":"30","attendee_emails":["ana@example.com","bo@example.com"]})`
C. BENCH_BFCL_C = `create_calendar_event({"title":"Mobile launch review","start_iso":"2026-07-02T14:00:00Z","duration_minutes":30,"attendee_emails":["ana@example.com","bo@example.com"]})`
D. BENCH_BFCL_D = `send_email({"subject":"Mobile launch review","to":["ana@example.com","bo@example.com"]})`
scenarios.description: A BFCL-inspired function-calling correctness item that does not require real tools.

scenarios.title: IFEval-style constraint following
scenarios.prompt:
Choose the candidate response that satisfies all constraints. Reply with exactly the token for the correct option and no other text.

Original instruction: Write exactly two sentences. Do not use commas. Include the word latency exactly once. End with the word done.

A. BENCH_IFEVAL_A = `Measure latency today. Then report done`
B. BENCH_IFEVAL_B = `Measure latency, then report done.`
C. BENCH_IFEVAL_C = `Measure latency today. Then report latency done`
D. BENCH_IFEVAL_D = `Measure today and report done`
scenarios.description: An IFEval-inspired verifiable-instruction item scored by selecting the compliant candidate token.

scenarios.title: Structured extraction
scenarios.prompt: Extract the values from the note and return one compact JSON object with keys token, project, priority, due_date, owners. Set token to {{expectedToken}}. Do not use Markdown.\n\nNote: Project Atlas has priority P1. It is due on 2026-07-15. Owners are Lina Chen and Omar Patel.
scenarios.description: A structured extraction item inspired by JSON/value-accuracy benchmarks.
